Access Point.definition: A networking hardware device that allows other Wi-Fi devices to connect to a wired network or wireless network.

actual argument.definition: The values or expressions provided to a function when it is executed.

adapter.definition: A device that allows one electrical device with different ports to connect with each other.

address.definition: A string assigned to a device connected to a computer network that uses the Internet Protocol for communication.

adware.definition: Malware that displays online advertisements in the user interface or on a screen for revenue.

aggregate function.definition: A function where multiple values are processed together to form a single summary statistic.

algorithm.definition: A sequence of code used to perform computation.

aliasing.definition: A phenomenon which bitmap images look pixelated when the image data is poorly reconstructed.

American National Standards Institute.definition: An organization that assigns unique ID for products.

American Standard Code for Information I.definition: A character encoding standard for representing a set of characters typically seen on an American keyboard.

analog.definition: Relating to signals or information represented by a physical variable.

Analog Digital Converter.definition: A system that converts an analog signal into a digital signal.

anchor.definition: An HTML tag that redirects the user to another hyperlink when clicked.

applet.definition: A small application that performs one specific task.

application program interface.definition: A connection between computer programs that offers a service to other pieces of software.

Application Service Provider.definition: A company that offers access to applications and related services over the internet.

argument.definition: A special variable used to provide data to a subprogram when it is called.

Arithmetic and Logic Unit.definition: A part of a CPU that performs arithmetic and bitwise operations.

array.definition: A data structure consisting of multiple elements (values or variables).

Artificial Intelligence.definition: The capability of computers to perform tasks typically associated with human intelligence.

assembler.definition: A computer program that takes in human code and converts them into machine code.

assembly language.definition: A low-level programming language that represents machine code strongly.

assignment statement.definition: An instruction to set a value to the variable name in a program.

attenuation.definition: The loss of signal strength in networking cables or connections.

attribute.definition: A field for each record in a database.

Augmented Reality.definition: A technology that overlays real-time 3D computer graphics onto a portion of the real world through a display.

bandwidth.definition: The maximum rate of data transfer through a period of time.

bar code.definition: A row of black bars with variable thickness that represents data in a machine-readable form.

Basic Input / Output System (BIOS).definition: A type of firmware that performs hardware initialization during the booting process.

translation: 成批處理；整批處理；成批工件處理
batch processing.definition: A processing method when computers processes data in a set time interval.

Beginning Of File.definition: The starting point of any data file used as a reference for file reading.

BIG-5 code.definition: A character encoding system for traditional Chinese characters.

bit.definition: The basic unit of information, represented by 1/0.

bitmap.definition: An image type formed from pixels.

bitwise complement.definition: A operation that flips the bits of a given binary value.

blog.definition: A website that consists of its owner's diary-style posts.

Bluetooth.definition: A technology that is used for exchanging data between fixed and mobile devices over short distances.

Boolean.definition: Data type that has one of two possible values: True/False

buffer.definition: A region of memory used to store data temporarily while it is being moved from one place to another.

bug.definition: A mistake in a program that results in an error in an algorithm.

bundled software.definition: A piece of software that comes with an operating system for free after purchase.

bus.definition: A pathway that transfers data between components inside a computer.

translation: 快取記憶體；高速緩衝器
cache.definition: A hardware or software component that stores data so that it can be retrieved faster later.

candidate key.definition: A potential field in a table that could be an identifier for a record.

translation: 級聯式頁面；層疊樣式表
Cascading Style Sheet.definition: A language used for specifying the styling of a HTML document.

Central Processing Unit.definition: A computer component that is responsible in arithmetic, logic, and input/output (I/O) operations.

Certificate Authority.definition: An organization that stores, signs, and issues digital certificates.

character.definition: A letter, number, or symbol used in strings.

check box.definition: Usually an answer field that only allows True/False for answering questions with multiple answers.

check digit.definition: A redundancy check used for error detection on identification numbers.

chip.definition: A piece of integrated circuit typically small in size.

client-side.definition: Refers to operations that are performed by the client in a computer network.

code.definition: A string of text used to communicate instructions for a computer.

command line interface.definition: An user interface that does not contain any graphics and contains purely of text.

Compact Disc.definition: a small plastic disk with a shiny surface on which information is recorded.

Compact Disc Read-Only Memory (CD-ROM).definition: A CD that can only be read, but not written to.

Compact Disc Recordable (CD-R).definition: A CD that can only be written once, but can be read multiple times.

Compact Disc Rewritable (CD-RW).definition: A CD that can be written multiple times.

compiler.definition: A computer program that translates computer code written in one programming language into another language.

console.definition: An interface that can be used for entering data into a computer.

constant.definition: A variable with an unassignable value.

cookie.definition: A small block of data created by a web server while a user is browsing a website.

crack.definition: To remove copy protection from a software.

cryptography.definition: The study of techniques for secure communication.

cursor.definition: An indicator used to show the position on a monitor that will respond to input.

data mine.definition: To extract and finding patterns in massive data sets.

translation: 數據冗餘度；數據冗餘性
data redundancy.definition: The existence of unnecessary data that permits correction of errors.

data structure.definition: A way of arranging data so that it can be accessed and updated efficiently.

database.definition: An organized collection of data on people, places or things.
